title: PowerShell 7-only operators (??, ?., ??=) break PS 5.1 compatibility
description: Null-coalescing (??), null-conditional (?.), and null-coalescing assignment (??=) operators were introduced in PS 7. Using them in code that must run under PS 5.1 causes parse errors. Already checked by SyntaxGuard (check #5) but this SIN formalises the scan-and-fix pattern.
ps_version_scope_note: These operators (??, ?., ??=) are valid and preferred in PS7.6 target code. Only flag when scan runtime is PS51 or Both.
detection_method: Regex scan for ?? and ?. operators outside of comment/string contexts
preventionRule: All code must remain PS 5.1 compatible. Use if ($null -ne $x) { $x } else { $default } instead of $x ?? $default.
remedy: Replace ?? with ternary if/else: if ($null -ne $val) { $val } else { $fallback }. Replace ?. with: if ($null -ne $obj) { $obj.Prop }.
